alkylation reagent: alkylated with 11 mM iodoacetamide (final concentration) for 45 min at room temperature in darkness.
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 11 mM iodoacetamide (final concentration) for 45 min at room temperature in darkness.
cleavage agent: trypsin was added at 1:50 trypsin-to-protein mass ratio for the first digestion overnight.
collision energy: peptides were then selected for MS/MS using normalized collision energy (NCE) setting as 28.
enrichment method: the crotonylation profiling of fish pathogen, S. agalactiae was investigated by combining affinity enrichment with LC MS/MS.
instrument: the peptides were subjected to nanospray ion source (NSI) source followed by tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) in Q ExactiveTM Plus.
ionization type: the peptides were subjected to nanospray ion source (NSI) source followed by tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS).
labeling: the crotonylation profiling of fish pathogen, S. agalactiae was investigated by combining affinity enrichment with LC MS/MS.
reduction reagent: the protein solution was reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol (final concentration) for 30 min at 56 °C.
reduction concentration: the protein solution was reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol (final concentration) for 30 min at 56 °C.
